leagueBriefing: Top-ranked programs showcased their power on Friday, as No. 1 UCLA, No. 2 LSU, and No. 3 Texas cruised to double-digit run-rule victories. Meanwhile, High Point shocked the college baseball world by snapping No. 9 Florida's 13-game winning streak.

games.headline: Cholowsky and Gasparino fuel top-ranked UCLA's 11-1 rout of Ohio State
games.brief: The top-ranked Bruins wasted no time asserting their dominance in their Big Ten debut, jumping out to an eight-run lead and cruising past Ohio State on Friday afternoon. Logan Reddemann was lights-out on the mound, keeping the Buckeyes off the board during his start and letting the Bruins' high-powered offense steal the show.
bullets: Logan Reddemann matched a career-high with 10 strikeouts over six scoreless innings.
Roch Cholowsky and Will Gasparino both launched two-run homers in a four-run second inning.
Mulivai Levu paced the offense with a four-hit day.
games.context: UCLA starts its first-ever Big Ten conference campaign with a commanding run-rule win, improving to 11-2.

games.headline: No. 2 LSU flexes offensive muscle in 15-4 demolition of Sacramento State
games.brief: Second-ranked LSU showed off its terrifying firepower, crushing Sacramento State 15-4 in a seven-inning run-rule rout. The Tigers recorded 14 hits and poured on the runs early to give their bullpen a mostly stress-free evening under the lights at Alex Box Stadium.
bullets: LSU ended the game early via the run rule in the seventh inning.
The Tigers scored 15 runs on 14 hits.
Sacramento State committed two errors and could not slow down the relentless LSU attack.
games.context: LSU validates its top-two national ranking with a completely overwhelming offensive performance, improving to 12-3.

games.headline: Pack Jr.'s grand slam ends night early as No. 3 Texas stays undefeated
games.brief: The third-ranked Longhorns continued their perfect start to the season with a 14-2 run-rule victory over USC Upstate on Friday night in Austin. Texas turned the game into a home run derby, launching five long balls including a game-ending grand slam by Anthony Pack Jr. in the eighth inning to secure the early finish.
bullets: Anthony Pack Jr. ended the game via run-rule with an eighth-inning grand slam, finishing with four RBIs.
Texas blasted five home runs on the night.
The Longhorns racked up 12 hits while holding the Spartans to just five.
games.context: Texas improves to a flawless 13-0 on the year, extending one of the best starts in program history.

games.headline: No. 4 Mississippi State pulls away from Lipscomb 9-4 behind steady offense
games.brief: Fourth-ranked Mississippi State handled business at home, defeating Lipscomb 9-4. The Bulldogs used a steady diet of timely hits to build a comfortable lead and let their talented pitching staff finish the job, comfortably putting away a pesky Bisons squad.
bullets: Mississippi State tallied 11 hits compared to Lipscomb's nine.
Lipscomb committed two errors that led to unearned Bulldogs runs.
The Bulldogs improved to 13-2 on the season.
games.context: Mississippi State comfortably takes care of a mid-major opponent to keep their early-season momentum rolling.

games.headline: Yellow Jackets swarm Hokies with historic 16-1 run-rule victory
games.brief: Fifth-ranked Georgia Tech continued its record-breaking offensive pace, dismantling Virginia Tech 16-1 in a weather-delayed, seven-inning ACC opener. The Yellow Jackets chased the Hokies' starter in the first inning after seven of the first eight batters reached base and never looked back.
bullets: Georgia Tech scored its most runs in an ACC opener since 1995.
Kent Schmidt returned from injury to drive in the first two runs of the game with a bases-loaded single.
The Yellow Jackets have outscored opponents 194-53 through 14 games.
games.context: Georgia Tech improves to 13-1, tying its best start since 2006 behind a terrifying offensive lineup.

games.headline: No. 6 Arkansas uses 13 hits to brush past Stetson 7-1
games.brief: Sixth-ranked Arkansas overcame some sloppy defensive play to secure a 7-1 victory over Stetson. The Razorbacks committed three errors, but their dominant pitching and 13-hit offensive output more than made up for the fielding miscues.
bullets: Arkansas pitching held Stetson to a single run on eight hits.
The Razorbacks won comfortably despite committing three errors in the field.
Arkansas improves to 11-3 on the young season.
games.context: Arkansas leans on superior arm talent and a deep lineup to overcome an uncharacteristically sloppy night in the field.

games.headline: No. 7 Auburn pitching stifles Winthrop in 10-0 shutout sweep
games.brief: The seventh-ranked Tigers relied on the long ball and stellar pitching to blank Winthrop 10-0 in a run-rule victory at Plainsman Park. Auburn's lineup tallied 11 hits and capitalized on early opportunities to cruise to a stress-free win in just seven innings.
bullets: Auburn secured a decisive 10-0 run-rule victory in just seven innings.
Winthrop was held to three scattered hits and shut out entirely.
The Tigers committed zero errors in a clean defensive performance.
games.context: Auburn improves to 11-2 on the season, showcasing both depth on the mound and power at the plate.

games.headline: Virginia routs No. 8 North Carolina 13-3 in massive ACC clash
games.brief: Virginia made a massive statement in Chapel Hill, blowing out eighth-ranked North Carolina 13-3 in a game shortened by the run rule. The Cavaliers battered the Tar Heels' pitching staff for 13 hits while UNC imploded defensively with three costly errors.
bullets: Virginia forced a run-rule finish against a top-10 conference rival.
North Carolina committed three costly errors in the blowout loss.
The Cavaliers improved to 12-2, establishing themselves as early ACC contenders.
games.context: Virginia delivers a stunning, lopsided road beatdown to a top-10 conference rival to shake up the ACC.

games.headline: High Point stuns No. 9 Florida to snap 13-game win streak
games.brief: High Point stunned ninth-ranked Florida with a late-inning surge, pulling out a 7-2 victory to snap the Gators' NCAA-best 13-game winning streak. Despite a brilliant start from Florida ace Liam Peterson, the Panthers rallied for five unanswered runs against the Gators' bullpen to secure the massive upset.
bullets: Wade Walton earned the win for High Point, allowing two runs over seven innings while striking out four.
Florida ace Liam Peterson took a no-hitter into the sixth, finishing with 11 strikeouts.
Lane Allen drove in two crucial runs for High Point with a single in the seventh inning.
games.context: High Point captures a huge non-conference win, handing Florida its first loss since Opening Night.

games.headline: No. 10 Southern Miss holds off North Alabama 9-4
games.brief: Tenth-ranked Southern Miss used efficient offense to defeat North Alabama 9-4 on Friday. Both teams tallied nine hits, but the Golden Eagles capitalized on high-leverage situations and free passes to maximize their run production.
bullets: Southern Miss scored nine runs despite tying North Alabama with nine hits.
The Golden Eagles committed two errors but stranded runners well.
Southern Miss improves to a stellar 12-2 on the season.
games.context: Southern Miss does what good teams do, turning equal hit totals into a comfortable five-run victory.

games.headline: No. 11 Georgia handles Queens University 12-2 in early finish
games.brief: Eleventh-ranked Georgia protected its home turf on Friday, overpowering Queens University for a 12-2 run-rule victory in eight innings. The Bulldogs offense generated eight hits and capitalized on clean execution to pull away early and rest their bullpen.
bullets: Georgia forced an eight-inning run-rule victory.
Queens University managed just five hits against the Bulldogs' pitching staff.
The Bulldogs played a clean game defensively with zero errors.
games.context: Georgia improves to 12-3 on the season as they continue to build momentum before deep conference play.

games.headline: Lafayette shocks No. 13 NC State with 11-6 road win
games.brief: Lafayette pulled off a massive upset, going into Raleigh and knocking off 13th-ranked NC State 11-6. The Leopards were incredibly efficient, scoring 11 runs on just eight hits while keeping the Wolfpack from putting together a massive comeback inning.
bullets: Lafayette scored 11 runs on only eight hits to secure the huge upset.
NC State actually out-hit Lafayette 10 to eight but fell short.
The Wolfpack suffer just their second loss of the season, falling to 12-2.
games.context: Lafayette secures a program-defining victory by shocking a top-15 ACC powerhouse on the road.

games.headline: No. 14 Clemson routs La Salle 14-1 in dominant showing
games.brief: Fourteenth-ranked Clemson made quick work of La Salle, cruising to a 14-1 run-rule victory in seven innings. The Tigers' offense exploded for double-digit runs early, providing more than enough cushion for their pitching staff to close things out quickly.
bullets: Clemson racked up 10 hits en route to 14 runs.
La Salle was limited to just four hits and a single run.
The Tigers secured the victory via the run-rule after seven innings.
games.context: Clemson improves to a dominant 12-1 on the season with a complete team performance.

games.headline: No. 15 Wake Forest uses 15 hits to beat Stanford 7-4
games.brief: Fifteenth-ranked Wake Forest unleashed a steady offensive attack to defeat Stanford 7-4 on Friday. The Demon Deacons racked up 15 hits, constantly threatening on the basepaths and eventually wearing down the Cardinal pitching staff.
bullets: Wake Forest recorded 15 hits to secure the three-run victory.
Stanford managed only six hits and committed one error.
The Demon Deacons improve to an impressive 13-1 on the season.
games.context: Wake Forest continues its dominant start to the season by handling a traditional West Coast power.

games.headline: No. 16 Coastal Carolina outlasts East Carolina in 10-inning thriller
games.brief: In a dramatic finish to a back-and-forth battle, 16th-ranked Coastal Carolina stunned East Carolina with a walk-off grand slam in the 10th inning to win 10-6. The Pirates had shown incredible resilience, erasing a 4-0 deficit, but the Chanticleers ended the night in extras with one swing of the bat.
bullets: Coastal Carolina secured the victory with a walk-off grand slam in the bottom of the 10th inning.
The Chanticleers racked up 13 hits compared to ECU's nine.
East Carolina fought hard but dropped to 7-6-1 on the year.
games.context: Coastal Carolina protects its home turf in a thrilling top-25 caliber matchup that sets the tone for the rest of the weekend series.

highlights.title: East Carolina vs Coastal Carolina highlights

games.headline: No. 17 TCU handles Tulane 10-2 to protect home turf
games.brief: Seventeenth-ranked TCU took care of business at home, defeating Tulane 10-2 with a complete performance. The Horned Frogs produced 12 hits and capitalized on two Green Wave errors to coast to a comfortable victory.
bullets: TCU offense tallied 12 hits en route to 10 runs.
Tulane committed two errors and managed only five hits.
TCU improves to 8-5 as they look to build momentum.
games.context: TCU grabs a solid, stress-free win to right the ship and protect their home field.

games.headline: No. 18 Oregon State holds off Xavier for 4-2 win
games.brief: Eighteenth-ranked Oregon State relied on clutch pitching and timely hitting to secure a tight 4-2 victory over Xavier. The Beavers' staff kept the Musketeers' bats quiet when it mattered most to grind out the home win.
bullets: Oregon State out-hit Xavier 8 to 3.
The Beavers' pitching staff limited Xavier to just two runs.
Oregon State improves to 8-4 on the young season.
games.context: Oregon State grabs a solid non-conference win by relying on foundational pitching.

games.headline: No. 19 Tennessee survives scare to edge Wright State 4-3
games.brief: Nineteenth-ranked Tennessee narrowly avoided an upset, holding off Wright State for a 4-3 victory. The Volunteers were actually out-hit by the Raiders and committed two errors, but found a way to scratch across the game-winning run when they needed it most.
bullets: Tennessee won despite being out-hit 10 to six by Wright State.
The Volunteers survived despite committing two defensive errors.
Tennessee improves to 11-3 with the gritty, ugly win.
games.context: Tennessee survives a major scare, proving they can win close games even when playing poorly.

games.headline: No. 20 Florida State handles Northern Kentucky 7-3
games.brief: Twentieth-ranked Florida State took care of business at home, defeating Northern Kentucky 7-3. The Seminoles' pitching was spectacular, holding the Norse to just a single hit throughout the entire contest to safely navigate the Friday night slate.
bullets: Florida State pitching allowed only one hit to the Northern Kentucky lineup.
The Seminoles offense produced 10 hits to secure the four-run win.
Northern Kentucky managed to score three runs despite their lack of hits.
games.context: Florida State improves to 11-2, showcasing elite arm talent against non-conference competition.
